MTCIC Online Business Registry

Staff Reference Guide

This guide is for registry staff and supervisors working in the Online Business Registry system. Use the sidebar to navigate to any procedure. Use the search box to find specific topics.

i
All references to "Document review" in older materials have been updated to Data review throughout this guide.

How to use this guide

  • Navigate using the sidebar on the left, or click the cards above to go to the most common procedures.
  • The Reference section contains lookup tables — they are not procedures but are linked from within procedures where relevant.
  • Items marked TO DO are placeholders where content is still to be confirmed.
  • Button labels shown in the system appear like this: SUBMIT ACTION SEND BACK FOR REVISION DENY
Overview

New to the registry?

This page is for staff who are new to the role. Read it before using any other part of this guide — everything else will make more sense once you understand the basics.

i
After reading this page, go to Filing flows overview to see how each type of filing moves through the system, then Data entry to begin your first filing.

What the registry is

The Kiribati Online Business Registry is the official list of all companies and business names registered in Kiribati. It is operated by the Ministry of Tourism, Commerce, Industry and Cooperatives (MTCIC) and maintained through the business.gov.ki website.

When a business registers, their details are recorded on the register and made available to the public. Keeping this information accurate and up to date is a legal requirement — and your job is to make sure that happens correctly.

What types of entities are on the register

Entity typeWhat it isForms used
Local companyA company incorporated in KiribatiCO- forms
Overseas companyA foreign company registered to operate in KiribatiCO- forms
Business nameA sole trader or partnership operating under a registered nameBN- forms

What your role involves

Your work falls into three main categories:

Daily

Processing filings

Clients submit forms through their online account. You check the information and enter it into the register.

Regular

Supporting clients

Approving new accounts, managing authority requests, topping up balances, responding to support issues.

Monthly

Compliance

Sending annual return reminders and breach notices to entities that have not filed on time.

Important things to know from day one

!
Never enter information that is not on a submitted form. If information looks wrong or incomplete, send it back to the client for correction.
  • The register is a legal record. Accuracy matters. If you are unsure about anything, stop and ask.
  • Some decisions must go to your supervisor or the Registrar. This guide will tell you when. When in doubt, ask before acting.
  • State-Owned Enterprises are different. If any request involves a State-Owned Enterprise, inform the Registrar before taking any action.
Overview

Technical issues

There are two types of technical issues you may encounter. The type determines who you contact and how.

Type 1 — Client support issues

i
Client support issues are questions or problems submitted by clients through the registry website. You answer these yourself using the Support Issues function. See Responding to client inquiries for the full procedure, and Support issue responses for guidance on how to respond to common questions.

Handle these yourself when a client:

  • Cannot find a form or feature on the website
  • Has a question about how to register or file
  • Has forgotten their username or password
  • Needs to know the status of their filing
  • Has a question about fees or payment
  • Needs help uploading documents

Type 2 — Platform technical issues

!
For platform technical issues, notify your supervisor first, then contact Paradigm Apps. Do not attempt to fix system or configuration issues yourself.

Escalate to your supervisor and then Paradigm Apps when:

  • The registry website is not loading or is showing an error
  • A form is not working correctly or is missing fields
  • A report is producing incorrect results or cannot be generated
  • You need a custom report that does not exist in the system
  • A system feature is not behaving as expected
  • Data appears to have been lost or corrupted

How to contact Paradigm Apps

Contact methodDetails
Support emailTO DO Paradigm Apps support email address
Support portalTO DO Paradigm Apps ticketing URL
Urgent issuesTO DO Phone or escalation contact

When logging an issue with Paradigm Apps

Provide as much detail as possible so the issue can be resolved quickly. Include:

  • What you were trying to do when the issue occurred
  • What you expected to happen
  • What actually happened (including any error messages — copy the exact wording)
  • The date and time the issue occurred
  • Your name and the registry (Kiribati MTCIC)
  • A screenshot if possible
i
If the website is completely unavailable and clients are unable to file, note the date and time the outage started. When the system is restored, inform any clients who attempted to contact the registry during the outage.
Overview

Filing flows overview

Each filing type follows a specific end-to-end sequence. Click a flow to see its steps.

Account creation New client account requests — several times weekly
▼

See: Account creation review

Re-registration Existing entities re-entering the register
▼

See: Account creation review · Authority requests · Data entry · Data review

New registration Brand new entities joining the register — authority granted automatically
▼

See: Data entry · Data review

Change filing Client-initiated updates to entity details
▼

See: Data entry · Data review · Form codes & tab guide

Restoration — standard Failure to file AR, or failure to re-register (companies and overseas companies only)
▼

See: Authority requests · Restoration

Restoration — exceptional Waiver of annual return approved, or failed re-registration
▼

See: Restoration

Annual return No staff involvement unless entity is non-compliant
▼

If non-compliant, see: Annual return cycle

Registry staff action Client or system Hold or exception
Overview

Service level standards

Service level standards are the maximum time it takes the registry to process a filing or client request. In most cases it will be possible to complete tasks faster.

Filing or client requestComplete within
Account creation1 working day
Grant authority over entity1 working day
Revoke authority over entity1 working day
Re-registration — all entities3 working days
Registration — all entities3 working days
Annual returns — all entitiesn/a (client self-service)
Change filings — all entities1 working day
Restorations — all entities5 working days
Overview

Roles & responsibilities

The registry has three internal user roles. Each role has different responsibilities and system permissions.

RoleResponsible for
Registry staff Data entry · Approving accounts · Managing authority requests · Topping up client balances · Managing client enquiries · Compliance activities
Registry supervisor Data review · Approving accounts for State-Owned Enterprises · Authority requests for SOEs · Client enquiries · Running reports · Compliance activities
Registrar Data review · Compliance activities
!
If any request relates to a State-Owned Enterprise, you must inform the Registrar before taking any action.

Dashboard tabs

TabPurposeUsed by
Data EntryFilings submitted by clients requiring data entry to create or update an entity recordRegistry staff
Data ReviewEntity records needing review and approvalSupervisor · Registrar
Entity Authority RequestsUsers requesting authority to file on behalf of an entityRegistry staff
My TasksTasks assigned to youAll
My EntitiesEntities you have permission to file on behalf ofAll
My FilingsFilings you have submittedAll
My OrdersOrders you have madeAll
i
In addition to the work queue, check the ADMIN menu regularly for new account requests under Approve New Accounts and for client enquiries under Support Issues.

Permissions by role TO DO

!
Detailed permissions for each role are to be confirmed with Paradigm Apps before go-live.
Core workflow

Data entry

i
This procedure applies to all filing types — re-registration, new registration, change filings, and restoration. The steps are the same regardless of which form was submitted. Refer to the Form codes and tab guide for form-specific details on which tabs to update and what to check.
i
You are completing or amending an entity record. The entity record contains all the key information that the registry holds on the business name or company. It is important that it is correct.

If the client has submitted information that appears incorrect or incomplete you should send back for revision. NEVER attempt to enter information that is not actually on the form.
!
A client must have submitted a filing through their online account before you can process it. You cannot create or change an entity record without a corresponding client filing.

Completing or changing an entity record

1
Log in and go to the Data Entry tab

Log in with your username and password. From the dashboard, select a task to action under the Data Entry tab, or click the Get Next Filing button to pick up the next item in the queue.

2
Review the filing

The system will display the entity record and automatically open the form submitted by the client.

  • For re-registrations: the record will prefill the entity name and registration number. You must still check and enter all data from the form.
  • For new registrations: the record will only have the proposed entity name completed.
  • For change filings: all entity record fields will be greyed out. Click the relevant question on the entity record to enable editing of that field.
  • For restorations: all entity record fields will be greyed out. See Restoration for more information.
No errors
Continue to step 3
Errors / problems
Click SEND BACK FOR REVISION — the client will be notified
3
Check name compliance (re-registrations, registrations and name changes only)

The system performs basic name checks automatically, but you must also review the name manually.

  • Check whether the name is identical or almost identical to another name in use.
  • See name compliance rules for the full requirements.
Name complies
Continue to step 4
Name does not comply
Consult a Registry Supervisor and/or the Registrar before proceeding. You may send back for revision or deny. If denying, communicate the applicant's appeal rights:
  • Company refusals: a person aggrieved by a decision by the Registrar may apply to the Court within 21 days after notification, or within any further time the Court may allow.
  • Business name decisions: the applicant may bring an appeal to the High Court within 28 days of notification of the decision.
If you deny an application you must record all communications with the applicant on the correspondence record.
4
Enter data and check uploads

Input the details from the form into the relevant tabs on the entity record. Refer to the Form codes and tab guide to confirm which tabs apply to the specific form being processed.

  • Check that all required supporting documents have been uploaded.
  • Check that upload quality is sufficient to read.
All correct
Continue to step 5
Errors / missing documents
Click SEND BACK FOR REVISION
5
Submit for data review

Once all details have been entered and uploads checked, click the SUBMIT button. The filing will be sent automatically for data review.

Reviewing work queue status

You can filter the work queue by status or workflow step to check progress on any filing.

1
Go to the Registry Work Queue

Select ADMIN from the main menu, then click REGISTRY WORK QUEUE.

2
Filter by status or workflow step

Use the Status dropdown to filter by filing status, or the Workflow Step dropdown to filter by stage. Click SEARCH to apply.

Core workflow

Data review

i
Data review can only occur after data entry has been completed. Data review is performed by the Registry Supervisor or Registrar.
1
Log in and go to the Data Review tab

Log in with your username and password. From the dashboard, select a task under the Data Review tab, or click the Get Next Filing button.

2
Open the scanned document

The amended entity record will be displayed. Click OPEN SCANNED DOCUMENT to open the form submitted by the client that was used to amend the entity record.

3
Review the entity record against the form

Check that what was entered in the entity record matches what the client submitted on the form. Check that all required documents are present and correct.

No errors
Click APPROVE STEP — the filing is complete
Errors found
Click SEND BACK TO DATA ENTRY — data entry staff will be notified to correct and resubmit
!
For restoration filings where the late annual return is required: do not approve the filing until data entry staff have confirmed that the client has submitted the required late annual return. See Restoration for details.
Supporting task

Account creation review

Service standard: 1 working day
!
If an account request relates to a State-Owned Enterprise, inform the Registrar before taking any action.
1
Go to Approve New Accounts

From the main menu, click ADMIN then APPROVE NEW ACCOUNTS.

2
Open the account task

Click the blue ACTION button next to the account you want to review.

3
Review account information and ID uploads

Check the account details and verify the identity documents uploaded by the applicant.

Acceptable
Click APPROVE ACCOUNT
Revision needed
Click SEND BACK FOR REVISION. You must enter a reason — this will be sent to the client.
Unacceptable
Click DENY ACCOUNT. You must enter a reason — this will be sent to the client.

Update user account details

!
Clients are expected to update their own account details. Only update a client account in exceptional circumstances.
1
Go to Accounts

From the main menu, click ADMIN then ACCOUNTS.

2
Search for the account

Enter details using any of the search parameters and click SEARCH.

3
Select and update

Click on the account you wish to update. Amend the details as necessary and click SAVE.

Deactivate a user account

!
Only deactivate a client account in exceptional circumstances.
1
Follow steps 1–2 above (Admin → Accounts → search → select account)
2
Go to the Users tab

In the Account Profile, select the Users tab.

3
Deactivate

Click the Deactivate button next to the relevant user. Select Yes in the confirmation dialogue.

Supporting task

Authority requests

Service standard: 1 working day
i

Authority requests are processed by Registry staff for re-registration and restoration requests. In most cases, authority over an entity is granted by an existing client who already has authority.

In exceptional circumstances, you can grant authority to a company officer (e.g. director) who has requested it from the existing authority holder but has not received a response.

Grant authority over an entity

1
Go to the Entity Authority Requests tab

Log in and click the Entity Authority Requests tab on the dashboard.

2
Open the request

Click the blue ACTION button to review the request.

3
Review information and ID upload
Acceptable
Click APPROVE REQUEST
Revision needed
Enter reason in the Entity Authority Review Note box (sent to client). Click SEND BACK FOR REVISION.
Unacceptable
Enter reason in the Entity Authority Review Note box (sent to client). Click DENY REQUEST.

Revoke authority over an entity

1
Find the entity record

Use the search function to locate the entity you wish to revoke authority over.

2
Go to the Entity Authority tab

Navigate to the ENTITY AUTHORITY tab on the entity record and click the orange Revoke button next to the relevant user.

3
Confirm revocation

Insert a reason for revoking authority and click CONFIRM.

Supporting task

Top up client balance

Service standard: 1 working day
!
Top up is only required where a client has made a cash payment to the MTCIC accounts office. The MTCIC accounts office must have sent the IFMIS receipt to the official registry email account before you proceed. TO DO Exact process to be confirmed.
1
Go to Fund Account / Payment

From the dashboard, click ADMIN. In the Administration Console, select FUND ACCOUNT / PAYMENT.

2
Search for the account

Click the green search for account button. Search using any of the available parameters.

3
Select the account

Click the blue Select button next to the account you want to top up.

4
Complete the Fund Account form

Complete all required fields and click SUBMIT.

FieldWhat to enter
Name of payerName of the person who paid at the MTCIC accounts office
Receipt numberIFMIS receipt number
Confirm receiptRe-type IFMIS receipt number
Receipt narration (optional)Leave blank
Date of paymentDate from the IFMIS receipt
Amount (AUD)Amount paid
Confirm amount (AUD)Re-type amount paid
UploadUpload the IFMIS receipt
5
Confirm

A confirmation dialogue will appear. Select Yes. The client will automatically receive an email notification that their balance has changed.

Manual correction of client balance

!
  • Registry credit must correspond to a cash payment evidenced by an IFMIS receipt.
  • Filing fees are not refundable under any circumstances.
  • Manual corrections must have strong justification recorded.

Follow steps 1–3 above to navigate to the Fund Account page and select the client. Then:

  • To increase a balance: click Credit Entry. Enter an entry narration (justification), the amount to increase by, and upload supporting documentation. Click SUBMIT and confirm.
  • To decrease a balance: click Debit Entry and follow the same steps.

Find a specific transaction

1
Go to Payments

In the Administration Console, select Payments.

2
Search and select

Use the search parameters and click SEARCH. Click the transaction you wish to view.

Supporting task

Responding to client inquiries

i
All client inquiries are received and responded to through the Support Issues function in the registry system. Do not respond to client inquiries by direct email. Responses sent through Support Issues are not required to be logged on the correspondence record.

Check ADMIN → Support Issues regularly for new inquiries. For guidance on how to respond to common questions, see Support issue responses.

1
Go to Support Issues

From the main menu, click ADMIN then select SUPPORT ISSUES.

2
Search for support issues

Click the SEARCH button to see all open support issues, or filter by category or status before searching.

3
Open the support issue

Click on the ID number of the support issue you want to view. The full support request will be displayed.

4
Review the request

Read the client's inquiry carefully. Check the Support issue responses reference table if you are unsure how to respond.

5
Add internal notes if needed

If you need to record anything for internal reference before responding, type your notes under Support Issue Registrar Notes. These are not sent to the client.

6
Resolve the issue

Click the RESOLVE button. The Support Issue Resolution dialogue box will appear.

7
Select a resolution type and write your response

Select the appropriate resolution type from the dropdown and type your message to the client. Click Resolve Support Issue to send.

8
Confirm

A confirmation message will appear confirming the response has been sent to the client.

Supporting task

Correspondence record

i

The correspondence record must be used to log all messages sent by MTCIC to clients, except:

  • System dialogue messages (e.g. automatic "return for revision" notifications)
  • Responses sent through the Support Issues function

The correspondence record logs that a message was sent. It does not itself send the message to the client.

Adding a correspondence record entry

1
Go to the entity profile

Navigate to the entity profile for the entity you have communicated with and select the CORRESPONDENCE tab.

2
Create a new record

Click the green + button to create a new correspondence entry.

3
Complete required fields and save
FieldWhat to enter
Correspondence directionSelect outwards
Correspondence typeSelect email (or other as appropriate)
Contact nameName of the person you communicated with
Contact emailEmail address of the person you communicated with
Correspondence date/timeDate and time the message was actually sent
NoteBrief description of what was sent, e.g. "Breach Notice #1 sent for failure to file annual return."
AttachmentUpload any relevant document (e.g. PDF of breach notice)

Click SAVE.

Responding to support issues

1
Go to Support Issues

Navigate to ADMIN then select SUPPORT ISSUES.

2
Search and select

Click SEARCH to see all support issues, or filter first. Click the ID number of the issue you want to view.

3
Review and add internal notes if needed

The support request will be displayed. You can record internal notes under Support Issue Registrar Notes.

4
Resolve

Click the green RESOLVE button. Select a resolution type from the dropdown and enter your message to the client. Click Resolve Support Issue.

For guidance on how to respond to specific support issue types, see the Support issue responses reference table.

Supporting task

Charging late fees

!
TO DO: Procedure to be confirmed with Paradigm Apps. Content will be added once the system process for applying late fees has been clarified.
Supporting task

Generate statistical reports

Reports are generated from the Administration Console. All report types follow the same steps — only the date parameters differ depending on the report.

How to generate a report

1
Go to Reports

In the Administration Console, select REPORTS.

2
Select the report and output format

Choose the report you want from the list and select the output format.

3
Set the date range

Enter a Start Date and, where applicable, an End Date. See the tables below for guidance on what date parameters each report uses.

4
Generate

Click the GENERATE REPORT button.

Compliance and administrative reports

ReportPurpose
All Entities – AR Allocated Month Shows all entities that are due to submit their annual return during the current calendar month.
All Entities – AR Non-Compliant Shows all entities that have not filed their annual return, including the date after which they must be removed from the Register.

Financial and administrative reports

ReportPurpose
User accounts Shows the number of user accounts by type.

Statistical reports

ReportPurpose and notes
NATIONAL: Registered Entities – All

Shows the status of all registered entities at a point in time.

Use when the question is: "How many entities did Kiribati have on [date]?"
NATIONAL: Registered Entities – New

Shows all new entities registered over a selected date range.

Use when the question is: "How many entities were registered between [date] and [date]?"

!
If an entity was registered and removed during the selected time frame, that entity may no longer exist on the register.
NATIONAL: Registered Entities Annual Report

Summarises key information over the course of a calendar year:

  • New registrations
  • Removed entities
  • New business activities
NATIONAL: New, Active and Removed Entities Shows the number of new, active, and removed entities by year.
Compliance

Annual return cycle

i

The annual return compliance cycle runs every month. You will receive a Google Calendar reminder at the start of each month telling you what to do. All emails are sent from the shared registry inbox.

Every registered entity has an allocated return month — the month in which they must submit their annual return each year. Each month, you run two system reports which will tell you which entities:

  • are due to submit their annual returns (entering the cycle)
  • are overdue and need to submit their annual return with a late fee (in the cycle)
  • are in full breach and need to be removed (removal process)

You use these reports to send notification emails. The system determines who gets which notice based on where the entity is in the annual return compliance cycle.

How the compliance cycle works

The diagram below shows what happens to an entity from the time their return is due. An entity can exit the cycle at any point by filing their annual return.

!
Entities that do not file must be removed from the register. Providing extensions or failing to remove the entity is not legally allowed.

File management — before you start each month

The Compliance Tracker is a Google Sheet that records which entities have received notices. You must create a fresh copy each month. Never edit the template directly.

!
Rule: the template is never edited. Every month you make a copy of it, rename it, and work in the copy.

Folder structure

AR Compliance
    _Templates
        Compliance Tracker — TEMPLATE
    2026
        May 2026
            Compliance Tracker
            AR Allocated Month report
            AR Non-Compliant report
        June 2026
            Compliance Tracker
            AR Allocated Month report
            AR Non-Compliant report

How to create this month's folder and tracker

1
Create this month's subfolder

Open Google Drive and go to AR Compliance → 2026 (or the current year folder). Create a new subfolder named [Month] [Year] (e.g. May 2026).

2
Copy the template

Go to AR Compliance → _Templates. Right-click on Compliance Tracker — TEMPLATE and select Make a copy.

3
Rename and move the copy

Rename the copy Compliance Tracker and move it into this month's subfolder.

4
Save reports to the same subfolder

When you download the two reports, save them into this month's subfolder.

5
Open the copy and begin working

Open the Compliance Tracker copy. Do not open the template.

i
If you are unsure whether you are in the template or a copy, check the filename at the top of the screen. It should show just Compliance Tracker — not TEMPLATE.

Start of month — what to do

i

At the start of every month you will receive a Google Calendar reminder.

Your tasks are the same every month: run two reports, then send emails.

The email tool determines which notice each entity receives based on how long they have been non-compliant.

1
Run the two reports

Log in to the registry. Go to ADMIN → REPORTS and download both of the following:

  • All Entities – AR Allocated Month — entities whose annual return is due this month.
  • All Entities – AR Non-Compliant — entities that have not yet filed and are overdue.

Save both files into this month's subfolder in Google Drive.

2
Open the email tool

Open the AR Compliance Google Sheet. You will see a custom menu called AR Compliance at the top of the screen.

Click AR Compliance → Import Reports from Drive.

The tool will read the two report files and prepare a list of entities for this month.

3
Prepare this month's emails

Click AR Compliance → Prepare This Month's Emails.

The tool will calculate which notice each entity should receive and populate the Review tab. Each row is colour-coded:

  • Green — pre-AR reminder (return due this month)
  • Yellow — Breach Notice 1 (one month overdue)
  • Orange — Breach Notice 2 (four months overdue)
  • Red — Breach Notice 3, final warning (six months overdue)
4
Review the list

Go to the Review tab and check the list. Each row has a Send? column set to YES.

Change YES to NO for any entity that should not receive an email this month (for example, if you know they have already filed but the system has not yet updated).

Do not change anything else in the list.

5
Send the emails

Click AR Compliance → Send Approved Emails.

The tool will send emails to all rows marked YES. A confirmation will appear when complete, showing how many emails were sent.

All emails are sent from the shared registry inbox. The sent folder is the record of what was sent and when.

6
Update the Compliance Tracker

Open this month's Compliance Tracker.

The tracker identifies where the entity is in the current month. The entity will remain in subsequent reports until it either files the outstanding annual return or is removed.

  • For entities that received Breach Notice 1 this month, add them to Tab 1 with their entity details and the date the notice was sent.
  • For entities that received Breach Notice 2 or 3, update their row with the date of the latest notice.
  • Remove any entities from the tracker that have since filed their annual return.
7
Update the correspondence record

For each entity that received an email, add an entry to the correspondence record in the registry system. Log it as an outward email. In the note field, record which notice was sent, for example:

"Breach Notice 1 sent for failure to file annual return."

See Correspondence record for instructions.

When to undertake the removal process

Each month, when you update the Compliance Tracker, check whether any entities have now reached the removal threshold — six months after Breach Notice 1 was sent.

!
How to identify entities for removal: in the Compliance Tracker, look at the date Breach Notice 1 was sent. If six or more months have passed and the entity has still not filed, they must be removed.

For the full removal process, refer to AR non-compliance removal.

Compliance

AR non-compliance removal

i
This procedure covers removal of entities that have failed to file their annual return after the full compliance cycle has been completed. Before proceeding, confirm that all three breach notices have been sent and that six months have passed since Breach Notice 1. See Annual return cycle.
!
Removal is not optional. Entities that have not complied after the full cycle must be removed. Providing extensions or failing to remove the entity is not legally allowed.
1
Confirm emails have been sent

Before removing any entity, confirm that all notices were sent correctly. In the Compliance Tracker, check that:

  • Breach Notice 1, 2, and 3 were all sent
  • At least six months have passed since Breach Notice 1
  • The entity has not filed their annual return

If any notices were not sent, do not proceed. Raise with a Registry Supervisor.

2
Find the entity and initiate a filing

Log in and search for the entity. Select the ONLINE FILINGS button on the entity record. Select Change of Entity Details and/or Shareholder Matters and click INITIATE FILING.

3
Change entity status to REMOVED

On the entity record, locate the Status field and change the value to REMOVED.

4
Select the reason for removal

When prompted, select Failure to file annual return from the reason dropdown.

5
Submit and confirm client notification

Click APPROVE STEP. The system will automatically send a notification email to the client confirming removal.

Update the correspondence record and record the removal date in the Compliance Tracker.

Occasional procedure

Restoration

Service standard: 5 working days

Restoration allows a removed entity to be reinstated to the register. Before proceeding, check Restoration eligibility to confirm the request is within the time limit and the person requesting has authority to do so.

i
People who had authority over an entity before it was removed will retain that authority after restoration. You only need to process an authority request if the person filing does not already have authority.

Which process applies?

Answer the questions below to find the correct process.

What type of entity is this?

Why was the business name removed?

Has the client also filed BN-3 to update their business information?

Has the client also filed BN-3 to update their business information?

Why was the company removed?

Has the client requested a waiver of the annual return requirement?

Has a supervisor approved the waiver?

When is a waiver appropriate? A waiver should only be approved where the person requesting restoration could not reasonably be expected to file an annual return — for example, a creditor, a receiver, a party to legal proceedings, or a person with an undischarged claim against the company. Directors and shareholders are generally expected to file and a waiver would not normally be approved for them.

Why was the overseas company removed?

Restoration with late annual return required

The client must file their outstanding annual return before the restoration can be finalised.

Restoration with late annual return required and BN-3

The client must file their outstanding annual return. BN-3 must also be processed as part of this restoration.

Restoration with annual return waived

The annual return requirement has been waived. The client does not need to file a late return.

Restoration after failure to re-register

The client must submit CO-16 and CO-0 at the same time.

Restoration after failure to re-register

The client must submit CO-22 and CO-0a at the same time.

Restoration with late annual return required

All entity types

The client must file their outstanding annual return before the restoration can be finalised.

1
Grant authority (if needed)

If the person requesting restoration does not already have authority over the entity, process the authority request first. See Authority requests.

2
Open the restoration filing

From the Data Entry tab, open the restoration filing (CO-16, CO-22, or BN-6).

Errors / docs missing
Click SEND BACK FOR REVISION
Conditions not met
Click REJECT — e.g. more than 2 years since removal for a company, more than 6 months for a business name
No issues
Click SUBMIT
3
STOP — hold before data review

Do not tell the data reviewer to proceed yet. The restoration must not be approved until the client has filed their late annual return.

The client will receive an automatic system email telling them to file within 14 days. Set a reminder to check back.

!
Do not release to data review until step 4 is complete.
4
Confirm the late annual return has been filed

Go to ADMIN → Reports and run the AR Non-Compliant report. If the entity no longer appears, the return has been filed and you can proceed. If it still appears, contact the client and wait further.

5
Release for data review

Tell the data reviewer they can now proceed to review and approve the restoration filing.

Restoration with late annual return required and BN-3

Business names only

The client must file their outstanding annual return before the restoration can be finalised. BN-3 must also be processed as part of this restoration.

1
Grant authority (if needed)

If the person requesting restoration does not already have authority over the entity, process the authority request first. See Authority requests.

2
Open the restoration filing and BN-3

From the Data Entry tab, open the restoration filing (BN-6) and the BN-3 together.

Errors / docs missing
Click SEND BACK FOR REVISION
Conditions not met
Click REJECT — e.g. more than 6 months since removal
No issues
Update entity record with revised information from BN-3, then click SUBMIT
3
STOP — hold before data review

Do not tell the data reviewer to proceed yet. The restoration must not be approved until the client has filed their late annual return.

The client will receive an automatic system email telling them to file within 14 days. Set a reminder to check back.

!
Do not release to data review until step 4 is complete.
4
Confirm the late annual return has been filed

Go to ADMIN → Reports and run the AR Non-Compliant report. If the entity no longer appears, the return has been filed and you can proceed. If it still appears, contact the client and wait further.

5
Release for data review

Tell the data reviewer they can now proceed to review and approve the restoration filing.

Restoration with annual return waived

Local companies only
i
The waiver must be approved by a supervisor before you begin this process. Appropriate for creditors, receivers, parties to legal proceedings, and others who could not reasonably be expected to file. Directors and shareholders would not normally qualify.
1
Grant authority (if needed)

If the person requesting restoration does not already have authority over the entity, process the authority request first. See Authority requests.

2
Open the restoration filing

From the Data Entry tab, open the restoration filing (CO-16).

Errors / docs missing
Click SEND BACK FOR REVISION
Conditions not met
Click REJECT
No issues
Click SUBMIT
3
Email the client to disregard the system notification

The system will automatically send the client an email telling them to file a late annual return. Because the annual return has been waived, the client does not need to do this. Send the client an email from the official registry inbox telling them to disregard that message. Update the correspondence record.

4
Release for data review

Tell the data reviewer they can proceed to review and approve the restoration filing.

Restoration after failure to re-register

Companies and overseas companies only
i
This situation is uncommon. It is strongly recommended that the client contacts registry staff before submitting their forms so they can be given the correct documentation requirements upfront.
1
Grant authority (if needed)

If the person requesting restoration does not already have authority over the entity, process the authority request first. See Authority requests.

2
Open the restoration and re-registration filings

The client must submit a restoration form and a re-registration form at the same time. From the Data Entry tab, open both filings together:

  • Local company: CO-16 + CO-0
  • Overseas company: CO-22 + CO-0a
Errors / docs missing
Click SEND BACK FOR REVISION
Conditions not met
Click REJECT
No issues
Update entity record per CO-0 or CO-0a. Set the return month to the current calendar month. Click SUBMIT
3
Email the client to disregard the system notification

The system will automatically send the client an email telling them to file a late annual return. This does not apply here. Send the client an email from the official registry inbox telling them to disregard that message. Update the correspondence record.

4
Release for data review

Tell the data reviewer they can proceed to review and approve the filings.

!
TO DO: Confirm with Paradigm Apps whether the system automatically sends a final notification to the client upon restoration approval, or whether this is a manual step.
Occasional procedure

Registrar-initiated correction

!
Registrar-initiated corrections are limited situations and must be documented and approved by a Registry Supervisor or the Registrar. See Removal categories for when correction is permitted.

How to correct the register (REG-0)

1
Find the entity

Log in and search for the entity you wish to amend.

2
Initiate a filing

Select the blue ONLINE FILINGS button on the entity record. Select Change of Entity Details and/or Shareholder Matters and click the green INITIATE FILING button.

3
Record justification

Under the General tab, type the reason you are amending the entity record.

4
Make the correction

Identify the relevant field on the entity record and make the required correction.

5
Approve

Click the blue APPROVE STEP button. A receipt will be displayed.

i
For removal of an entity, see Entity removal.
Occasional procedure

Entity removal

!
Removal is not optional. Changing entity status does not delete data — it changes the status only.

Answer the questions below to find the correct process. For a full list of removal grounds and legal references, see Removal categories.

What type of entity is this?

What is the reason for removal?

What is the legal ground for removal?

What is the reason for removal?

What is the legal ground for removal?

What is the reason for removal?

What is the legal ground for removal?

AR non-compliance removal

The full compliance cycle must be complete before proceeding — all three breach notices sent and six months passed since Breach Notice 1.

Voluntary cessation — BN-5

Process BN-5 through the standard data entry procedure. Set entity status to REMOVED on the date specified in the filing.

Voluntary dissolution — CO-14

Process CO-14 through the standard data entry procedure. Set entity status to REMOVED on the date specified in the filing.

Voluntary cessation of operations — CO-24

Process CO-24 through the standard data entry procedure. Set entity status to REMOVED on the date specified in the filing.

Registrar-initiated removal — business name

Note the legal ground above — you will need it for the REG-0 form and compliance register.

Registrar-initiated removal — company / overseas company

Note the legal ground above — you will need it for the compliance register and public notice.

Voluntary cessation — BN-5

Business names
1
Process the BN-5 filing

Open the BN-5 from the Data Entry tab and process it per the standard data entry procedure.

2
Set entity status to REMOVED

Change the entity status to REMOVED. Use the date specified in the BN-5 filing — not today's date.

3
Submit for data review

Click SUBMIT. The filing will move to data review for approval.

Voluntary dissolution — CO-14

Local companies
1
Process the CO-14 filing

Open the CO-14 from the Data Entry tab and process it per the standard data entry procedure.

2
Set entity status to REMOVED

Change the entity status to REMOVED. Use the date specified in the CO-14 filing — not today's date.

3
Submit for data review

Click SUBMIT. The filing will move to data review for approval.

Voluntary cessation of operations — CO-24

Overseas companies
1
Process the CO-24 filing

Open the CO-24 from the Data Entry tab and process it per the standard data entry procedure.

2
Set entity status to REMOVED

Change the entity status to REMOVED. Use the date specified in the CO-24 filing — not today's date.

3
Submit for data review

Click SUBMIT. The filing will move to data review for approval.

Registrar-initiated removal — business name

Business names · Registrar-initiated
!
Consult the Registrar and the MTCIC legal officer before proceeding. This is a contestible decision — you must be on solid legal footing and have explicit authorisation before taking any action. Do not begin this procedure without written confirmation from both.
i
This is an appealable decision. If a court appeal succeeds, the business name owner must submit BN-8 with the court order. Record the legal ground in all correspondence and filings.
1
Complete REG-0

Initiate a REG-0 filing. Record the legal ground for removal in the justification field. Change entity status to REMOVED and click APPROVE STEP.

2
Send Notice of Removal of Business Name from Register

Send the notice to the business name owner from the shared registry inbox. Update the correspondence record.

Registrar-initiated removal — company or overseas company

Companies and overseas companies · Registrar-initiated
!
Consult the Registrar and the MTCIC legal officer before proceeding. This is a contestible decision — you must be on solid legal footing and have explicit authorisation before taking any action. Do not begin this procedure without written confirmation from both.
1
Create a compliance register entry

Record: entity type · entity number · entity name · contact · email · legal ground for removal · specified compliance date.

2
Issue public notice (by Registrar)

Complete the "Registrar notice of intention to remove company or companies from the Kiribati register" and have it signed by an authorised person. Record the legal ground on the notice. Upload the public notice to the registry website. TO DO Instructions for publishing to be confirmed. Update the compliance register with the publication date.

3
Notify the responsible person

Send a copy of the public notice to the person with authority or a current director from the shared registry inbox. Update the correspondence record. At least one attempt must be made even if contact details are unavailable.

4
Remove the entity

After the notice period has expired and no response has been received, change entity status to REMOVED via a REG-0 filing.

Reference

Form codes & tab guide

Use this table to find the correct entity record tab(s) to update and any special notes for each form. All change filings follow the same data entry process — only the tabs and checks differ.

FormTitleAmend tabNotes
Companies — local
CO-0Application for re-registration of local companyAll tabsPre-filled — check and confirm all data from the form.
CO-1Application for incorporation of local companyAll tabsNew entity — check name compliance. At least one director must be a Kiribati resident.
CO-2Application to change name of Kiribati companyGeneralConfirm new name complies with requirements. See Name compliance rules.
CO-3Notice of substitution, adoption, or amendment of company constitutionGeneral
CO-4Notice of issue of sharesShares · Shareholders
CO-5Notice of company's acquisition of own sharesShares · Shareholders
CO-6Notice of redemption of redeemable sharesShares · Shareholders
CO-7Notice of transfer of sharesShareholders
CO-8Notice of change in directors or change in director detailsDirectorsAt least one director must be a Kiribati resident.
CO-9Notice of court order altering or adding to constitutionGeneralChange General tab if the order means the company is no longer using the default constitution. Details of the alteration must be on the entity record.
CO-10Notice of change in registered address, postal address, or principal place of businessAddresses
CO-11Notice of location of records not kept at registered officeNo tab changeNo entity record update required, but the filing must be recorded on the entity record.
CO-12Notice of removal or resignation of auditor appointed by RegistrarNo tab changeNo entity record update required, but the filing must be recorded on the entity record.
CO-13Notice of dissolution of Kiribati companyGeneralChange status to IN DISSOLUTION.
CO-14Request for removal from Kiribati register after dissolutionGeneralChange status to REMOVED.
CO-15Notice of court approved amalgamation, arrangement, or compromiseGeneralChange status to AMALGAMATED. Create new entity record for amalgamated company. TO DO
CO-16Application to restore company to Kiribati register—See Restoration procedure.
CO-17Notice of appointment, change of details, or cessation of auditor appointed by companyNo tab changeNo entity record update required, but the filing must be recorded on the entity record.
CO-18Notice of change of business activityBusiness activities
CO-19Notice of change of details of shareholdersShareholdersCheck if additional shareholder form has been uploaded. Check if upload for share structure explanation.
Companies — overseas
CO-0aApplication for re-registration of overseas companyAll tabsPre-filled — check and confirm all data from the form.
CO-20Application for registration of overseas companyAll tabsNew entity — check name compliance.
CO-21Notice of change of name of overseas companyGeneralConfirm new name complies with requirements.
CO-22Application to restore overseas company to Kiribati register—See Restoration procedure.
CO-23Notice of change in directors or change in director details of overseas companyDirectors
CO-24Notice by overseas company of ceasing to carry on business in KiribatiGeneralChange status to REMOVED on the date specified in the filing.
CO-25Notice of change of registered address, postal address, or principal place of business of overseas companyAddresses
CO-26Notice of change of shareholder details for overseas companyShareholders
CO-27Notice of change of business activity of overseas companyBusiness activities
Business names
BN-0Application for re-registration of business nameAll tabsPre-filled — check and confirm all data from the form.
BN-1Application for registration of business nameAll tabsNew entity — check name compliance.
BN-2Application to change month of annual returnGeneralDo not allow a change that effectively gives the business an extended period without filing a return. E.g. a business with a return month of October 2026 must not be allowed to apply in September 2026 to change their return month to August.
BN-3Notice of change of business informationVariesReview the content of the form and update the entity record as applicable.
BN-4Application to change name of registered business nameGeneralCheck name complies with requirements. See Name compliance rules.
BN-5Notice of cessation of business under business nameGeneralChange status to REMOVED on the date specified in the filing.
BN-6Application for restoration of business name after removal—See Restoration procedure.
BN-7Application to correct the registerVariesReview the form content and change the entity record as applicable.
BN-8Notice of court order concerning business name registrationVariesReview the court order and make changes as applicable. All orders must be kept on the entity record.
Registrar-initiated
REG-0Correct entity details / record removalVariesUsed for: correct details of a company · correct details of an overseas company · correct details of a business name · remove entity for failing to file annual return. Must be approved by Registry Supervisor or Registrar.
Reference

Name compliance rules

Apply these rules when processing new registrations, re-registrations, and name change filings. The system performs basic automatic checks, but you must also review manually.

Companies and overseas companies (s.11, Companies Act 2021)

A company or overseas company name must:

  • End with the word "Limited" or "Ltd"
  • Not contravene any enactment
  • Not be identical or almost identical to the name of another company
  • Not be offensive or likely to mislead or deceive (in the opinion of the Registrar)
!
Additionally, a company must not use the name of another company that has been removed from the register in the last 2 years.

Business names (s.16, Business Names Act 2021)

A business name must not:

  • Suggest that the business enjoys the patronage of the Government, a local authority, or a foreign state
  • Be the same or almost the same as: a currently registered business name; the actual name of a body corporate (unless the body corporate is the applicant); a name for which another entity has filed an application that is yet to be determined
  • Be a prescribed name
  • Be the same or almost the same as a previously registered business name removed within the last 6 months — unless the former registrant consents

The Registrar must refuse to register if there are reasonable grounds to believe:

  • The applicant is unlikely to carry on a business under the name
  • Use of the name would contravene another written law
  • Use of the name would infringe another person's intellectual property rights (including trademarks)
  • The name is likely to mislead, deceive, or offend the public
i
If a name does not comply, consult a Registry Supervisor and/or the Registrar before proceeding. If denying the application, communicate the applicant's appeal rights under the Companies Act 2021 or Business Names Act 2021 as appropriate.
Reference

Removal categories

Use the Procedure column to go directly to the relevant procedure for each removal category.

Companies and overseas companies

#SituationFormPublic noticeProcedure
1Failed to submit annual returnNo form✗AR non-compliance removal
2Does not comply with s.6 Companies Act 2021No form✓ RegistrarEntity removal — company other reasons
3In liquidation 6+ months, no IN-XX receivedNo form✓ RegistrarEntity removal — company other reasons
4Liquidation complete, prescribed notice receivedIN-XX TO DO✓ LiquidatorEntity removal — company other reasons
5Client requests removal after dissolutionCO-14✓ ClientEntity removal — CO-14
6Overseas company ceasing operations in KiribatiCO-24✓ ClientEntity removal — CO-24

Public notice means: publication in the National Gazette, publication on the registry website, or announcement on Radio Kiribati during business hours.

Business names

#SituationFormNotice requiredProcedure
7Failed to submit annual returnNo form✗AR non-compliance removal
8Owner files notice of cessation of businessBN-5✗Entity removal — BN-5
9Failed to notify change of business info within 28 daysNo form✓ RegistrarEntity removal — BN other reasons
10Failed to respond to s.24 noticeNo form✓ RegistrarEntity removal — BN other reasons
11Owner is body corporate in liquidation or deregisteredVaries✓ RegistrarEntity removal — BN other reasons

When correction of the register is permitted

#SituationRelates toNext step
Companies Act
1Registrar satisfied that information has been wrongly entered in or omitted from the registerOnly the person who provided the informationCorrect register
Other information1) Give notice of rectification 2) Correct register
2Particulars have been incorrectly enteredOnly the person who provided the informationCorrect register
Other information1) Give notice of rectification 2) Correct register
3Registrar has given notice to a company requiring corrected or updated details, or a certified copy of a documentAny informationCorrect register
Business Names Act
4Registrar satisfied that an entry contains an error or is otherwise incorrectAny information1) Give notice of rectification 2) Correct register
Reference

Restoration eligibility

Restoration can only be processed for the reasons and within the deadlines listed below. Check eligibility before beginning the Restoration procedure.

After failure to file annual return

EntityRemoval reasonRestoration deadline
CompanyFailure to file annual returnWithin 2 years from date of removal
Overseas companyFailure to file annual returnWithin 2 years from date of removal
Business nameFailure to file annual returnWithin 6 months from date of removal
Business nameFailure to file notice of change of business informationWithin 6 months from date of removal

After failure to re-register

EntityRemoval reasonRestoration deadline
CompanyFailure to re-registerWithin 2 years from date of removal
Overseas companyFailure to re-registerWithin 2 years from date of removal

Business names cannot be restored after failure to re-register.

Who can be granted authority for a restoration request

EntityWho can be granted authorityEvidence required
Company
Director (at time of removal)Director ID consistent with entity record
Shareholder (at time of removal)ID consistent with entity record, or ID + copy of entry from internal share register
CreditorID + evidence of being a creditor at time of removal
Entitled personID + copy of constitution confirming entitlement
LiquidatorID + copy of appointment paperwork
Party to a legal proceeding against companyID + evidence of legal proceeding
Person with undischarged claim against companyID + evidence of undischarged claim
Receiver of company propertyID + evidence of appointment as receiver
Overseas company
Director (at time of removal)Director ID consistent with entity record, or ID + evidence of appointment
Shareholder (at time of removal)ID + copy of entry from internal share register
Business name
Person registered to use the business nameID consistent with entity record
Authorised representativeID + evidence of authority to act on behalf of registered person
Reference

Support issue responses

Indicative guidance for responding to common client support issues. Use the Responding to client inquiries procedure to respond — not email directly.

CategoryClient issueIndicative action
Payment
PaymentI paid online but the transaction failedCheck the transaction record for the client. If the filing went through, inform the client. If the transaction did not go through but the client insists payment was deducted, refer the client to their bank.
PaymentHow do I pay? / I want to pay in cashDirect client to information on the website.
PaymentMy account was topped up for the wrong amountReview the IFMIS receipt on file and take corrective action if necessary.
PaymentI topped up the wrong accountTO DO Confirm process for correcting balance across multiple accounts.
PaymentSOEs don't have to pay because they are Government entitiesInform client there is no discretion to waive payments.
PaymentI would like a fee waiver / waiver of late filing feeTO DO Confirm waiver policy and response.
Transaction
TransactionWhere do I find the form to create an entity / file a change?Direct client to the forms page on the website.
TransactionCan you process my paper form so I don't have to do it online?Inform client that all transactions must be done through their client account.
TransactionDid my filing go through?Direct client to their filing history under the "My Entities" tab.
TransactionWhy is my filing taking so long?Locate the filing in the work queue and prioritise if it is outside the service standard.
TransactionI want to change information but there is no form (e.g. name change, gender change)Liaise with client to understand the exact request. Use a registrar-initiated change filing with full justification.
Technical assistance
TechnicalMy internet is too slow to upload documentsSuggest client reduces file quality to reduce file size.
TechnicalI do not have access to a printerInform client that forms can be filled in on a computer and uploaded directly without printing. Offer to print the form for the client.
TechnicalI can't understand the site in EnglishTO DO
Other
OtherWhere can I find general information about starting a business?Refer client to website content.
OtherI am listed as a director but did not consent to be oneRequest a copy of written consent from the company. If consent is not provided, update the entity record with justification.
OtherHow do I sign a document electronically?TO DO
Password / account access
PasswordI've forgotten my usernameRefer client to the forgotten username page on the website.
PasswordI've forgotten my passwordRefer client to the forgotten password page on the website.
AccountI want to update persons authorised to file for my businessDirect client to the External User Manual.
AccountAnother user revoked my authority without justificationTO DO
